name: South of the Border Chili
recipeIngredient: 1 red or green bell pepper, diced
15 ounces red kidney beans, rinsed and drained
2 tablespoons grated low-fat cheddar cheese, optional
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat, heat the oil. Add the ground turkey and cook, stirring to break up large pieces until lightly browned, about 5 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, remove the turkey from the skillet and set aside.
2. To the same skillet, add onion, bell pepper, and garlic. Cook until the vegetables are tender-crisp, about 4 to 5 minutes. Add chili powder and cook, stirring, for an additional minute.
3. Stir in kidney beans, stewed tomatoes, 1/4 cup water, and salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until thickened, about 10 to 12 minutes.
4. Halve the potatoes lengthwise, cutting almost to the base of each potato. Mash slightly with a fork, leaving in the skins. Spoon the chili mixture over each potato, dividing evenly. Top with cheddar cheese and sour cream.
Makes 4 servings. Preparation time: About 20 minutes. Cooking time: About 25 minutes.
Per serving: Approximately 485 calories, 30 grams protein, 58 grams carbohydrates, 15 grams fat, 28% of calories from fat, 72 milligrams cholesterol, 1050 milligrams sodium, 14 grams fiber.
